guide.title: Guide
guide.description: Learn how to connect to MCP servers registered in Crucible.
guide.connectionFlowDesc: Crucible's MCP servers are accessible via SSE (Server-Sent Events) or Streamable HTTP endpoints. You can connect from any MCP-compatible client.
guide.step1Desc: On the Servers page, find the endpoint URL of the server you want to use and copy it with the copy button. Depending on the server, the endpoint will be either /sse (SSE) or /mcp (Streamable HTTP).
guide.step2Title: Step 2 — Register with your MCP client
guide.step2Desc: Add the copied endpoint URL to your MCP client's configuration. Below are setup examples for popular clients.
guide.claudeDesktopDesc: Claude Desktop does not support direct remote MCP connections. Use mcp-remote to bridge stdio → HTTP.
guide.prerequisiteDesc: Node.js v20 or later is required. Run
guide.checkPaths: Check the paths for Node.js and mcp-remote.
guide.warningDesc: Use the full paths for node and proxy.js instead of npx mcp-remote. In nvm environments, npx may use an older Node.js version causing errors. The --allow-http flag is required for HTTP connections.
guide.claudeCodeDesc: Supports SSE / Streamable HTTP directly — add with a single command.
guide.cursorDesc: Supports SSE / Streamable HTTP directly. Add from Settings → MCP Servers.
guide.windsurfDesc: Supports SSE / Streamable HTTP directly. Add the endpoint URL from the settings screen.
guide.otherClientsDesc: Any MCP client that supports SSE or Streamable HTTP transport can connect by specifying the endpoint URL.
guide.troubleNode: Your Node.js version may be too old. Check that node --version shows v20 or later. In nvm environments, npx may use an unintended Node version, so specifying the full path is recommended.
guide.troubleHttp: Add --allow-http to the mcp-remote arguments. This flag is required for HTTP connections to non-localhost hosts.
guide.troubleDisconnect: Make sure Crucible is running. You can test endpoint reachability by running curl http://localhost:<port>/mcp (or /sse) from the terminal.
guide.endpointNote: Use the exact URL shown on the Servers page

about.title: About Crucible
about.subtitle: Crucible — a place where diverse MCP servers come together, transforming into the power of AI agents.
about.originDesc1: A crucible is a vessel for collecting various materials and creating new value. Like a crucible that gathers diverse materials, Crucible consolidates various MCP server tools into one place, creating an environment where AI agents can freely utilize them.
about.originDesc2: The MCP ecosystem has platforms like Smithery (a forge). Crucible also has roots in metallurgy — before tools are forged at the smithy, materials come together in the crucible. Crucible is that first step.
about.approachDesc1: Unlike open platforms such as Smithery or Tooluniverse, Crucible aims to let each user or group set up their own crucible in a closed environment.
about.approachDesc2: Not a large shared furnace, but a small crucible you can keep close at hand. Manage your own MCP servers on your own infrastructure, empowering your own AI agents — that's the sovereign MCP platform that Crucible provides.
about.logoDesc: The Crucible logo depicts a server bent into a U-shape to represent a crucible, with circles inside representing MCP servers. It symbolically shows diverse servers gathering in one vessel.
